description_long: This is a lifelike representation of a male Mallard duck, characterized by its iridescent green head, yellow bill, and rich brown chest. The body is robust and rounded, with a smooth gradient from brown to gray along the wings and back, and a distinct white ring separating the head from the chest. The tail feathers are short and slightly curled, with black and white markings visible from the rear. The legs and webbed feet are orange, positioned beneath the body for a natural, upright stance. The surface appears feathered but with a slightly glossy finish, suggesting a healthy, well-groomed bird. The overall proportions and coloration are true to a typical Mallard found in urban parks and waterways.
description: A realistic male Mallard duck with a green head, yellow bill, brown chest, and orange webbed feet, standing upright. The body features smooth feather textures and natural coloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the green head, yellow bill, and both orange webbed feet, with the chest and part of the wings visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the iridescent green head, yellow bill, brown and gray wing feathers, and one extended orange foot.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back, tail feathers, and the symmetry of the wings, with the head and neck visible from behind.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the green head, yellow bill, brown chest, and the other orange foot, with detailed wing feathering.
